# SKActionTimingMode.easeOut

**Case**

Specifies ease-out pacing. Ease-out pacing causes the animation to begin quickly and then slow as it completes.

## Declaration

```swift
case easeOut
```

## Discussion

By creating two separate actions, a [doc://com.apple.spritekit/documentation/SpriteKit/SKAction/moveTo(x:duration:)] and a [doc://com.apple.spritekit/documentation/SpriteKit/SKAction/moveTo(y:duration:)], and setting the former to [doc://com.apple.spritekit/documentation/SpriteKit/SKActionTimingMode/easeOut], you can visualize the effect of this timing mode by tracing the path of a circular shape node running the actions in a group:



## Constants

- **SKActionTimingMode.linear**: Specifies linear pacing. Linear pacing causes an animation to occur evenly over its duration.
- **SKActionTimingMode.easeIn**: Specifies ease-in pacing. Ease-in pacing causes the animation to begin slowly and then speed up as it progresses.
- **SKActionTimingMode.easeInEaseOut**: Specifies ease-in ease-out pacing. An ease-in ease-out animation begins slowly, accelerates through the middle of its duration, and then slows again before completing.
